subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Article IV, OECD, WJP, RSF, TI) + UK official (ONS, Bank of England, OBR) + UK independent press (BBC, FT, The Guardian).
Source-lean: moderate Anglophone (UK is Anglophone); wires framed the Reform-UK surge and protest crackdown more dramatically than multilateral sources.
ONS statistical credibility is a genuine, ongoing drag (Labour Force Survey still 'in development'); fiscal headroom is thin.

economic.notes: A credible central bank, a reserve currency, and strong banks, against high debt, persistent deficits, and thin fiscal headroom.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~94% GDP (highest since the early 1960s); deficit ~4.2% and improving but persistent; OBR headroom only ~0.6% of GDP, keeping tax/budget pressure high. (IMF Art IV May-2026; ONS; OBR)

monetary_independence.notes: An independent, credible Bank of England with full sovereign monetary control. (Bank of England)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Sterling is a global reserve currency (~5% of FX reserves, #3-4) - a real, modest structural benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Eased from the peak but sticky and above target (~2.8%, Apr-2026), projected to rise on energy/food. (BoE; ONS)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised (CET1 ~14.5%), strong liquidity, passed the 2025 stress test; strong, well-regulated global banks. (BoE FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Services/finance export strength and stable, predictable policy, no resource curse; offset by post-Brexit goods-trade friction. (ONS; OBR)

institutional.notes: Strong common-law courts, with a dented official-statistics credibility and concentrated press ownership.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong independent common-law courts and secure property/contract (WJP 15/143); civil-justice timeliness a watch-item. (WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: The ONS is independent but its credibility is dented: the Labour Force Survey remains 'in development' and a 2025 review faulted ONS performance - a genuine, ongoing drag. (OSR; ONS)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable, professional Whitehall, with visible delivery weaknesses (HS2 governance, ONS). (OECD; NAO)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 20th (2025); drags are concentrated national-title ownership, SLAPPs, and police-surveillance/riot-coverage incidents. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Stable institutions and a large majority, but thin governing legitimacy and migration-driven social strain.

political_stability.notes: Stable institutions and a large Labour majority (Jul-2024), but very low government approval, a polarizing Reform UK lead in polls, and fragmentation; transfers peaceful. (Ipsos; Opinium)

civil_liberties.notes: A strong baseline, but real tightening: a contested terror-law proscription (ruled unlawful Feb-2026) and protest-law expansion. (HRW; OHCHR; BBC)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful, durable skilled routes (Skilled Worker, Global Talent) but costly (fees + NHS surcharge) and tightening: a 2025 White Paper raises the skill floor and lengthens settlement to 10 years. Open but narrowing. (Commons Library; GOV.UK)

social_cohesion.notes: Aug-2024 anti-asylum riots (the worst in a decade), asylum-hotel flashpoints, and low institutional trust. (British Future; BBC)

demographics.notes: Ageing, with deaths set to exceed births from mid-2026; population still grows via migration, now falling sharply. (ONS)

geopolitical.notes: A top-tier security pillar; no direct conflict.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO/Five Eyes/AUKUS core, a nuclear power, and a UN Security Council permanent member. (NATO; FCDO)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict and sustained Ukraine support; elevated global-risk exposure. (UCDP)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An active sanctions regime; no capital controls; a global financial hub. (HM Treasury)

physical_practical.notes: Lower climate exposure and a functional flood-insurance backstop, against a deeply strained (if improving) NHS and ageing infrastructure.

climate.notes: Mild maritime, high readiness, low heat exposure - but among the most AMOC-exposed states; a slowdown could sharply cool/wet NW Europe (flag the tail). (ND-GAIN; IPCC AMOC)

healthcare.notes: The NHS is strained but improving off a deep trough (waiting list down to ~7.1m, interim targets hit); universal but pressured. (NHS England; BMA)

infrastructure.notes: Mature networks but ageing; HS2 is a flagship cost/schedule failure raising delivery-capacity concerns. (HS2 Ltd; NAO)

crime_safety.notes: Low homicide (~1.0/100k, falling) and knife crime down, though knife crime remains a real urban concern. (ONS)

disaster_insurance.notes: Flood Re (state-backed reinsurance) keeps high-risk flood cover available and affordable to 2039; functional, premiums rising. (Flood Re; ABI)

summary: Strong common-law institutions, a credible central bank and reserve currency, well-regulated banks, and a top-tier security role (NATO/Five Eyes/AUKUS, nuclear, UNSC). The drags are high debt with persistent deficits and thin fiscal headroom, dented official-statistics credibility, thin governing legitimacy amid a polarizing political surge, migration-driven social strain, and a deeply strained (if improving) NHS with ageing infrastructure.
